title: Ever had a change of heart
display_title: Ever had a change of heart
wikitext:
[]: Ever had a change of heart?             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Sun Jul 26, 2015 11:16 am was there ever something of EVA (an episode, a character, one of the movies, a manga chapter etc.) that you once disliked, but have now have come to appreciate?

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Sun Jul 26, 2015 11:22 am ^ I once thought Yui was an okay lady with just some flaws, but now I just view her like soldiers did, I appreciate her evil.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jul 26, 2015 11:40 am My view of Asuka has changed from "dislike" to "dislike and pity". Does that count?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jul 26, 2015 11:53 am It happened while I was watching the series -- going from wishing the annoying redhead had toasted in the volcano to her being the character I felt for the most while watching episode 16.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Jul 26, 2015 12:32 pm Oh, of course. I went from thinking the way the show ended was shite to considering it one of the better endings of any anime I've seen.

[]:             Quote Post by IronEvangelion » Mon Jul 27, 2015 12:31 am NemZ wrote: My view of Asuka has changed from "dislike" to "dislike and pity".

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jul 27, 2015 6:09 am After viewing The End of Evnagelion for the first time, I hated the entire series from beginning to end. I hated Episode 1 all the way to both versions of Episode 26. Then after a couple of days either something clicked or I finally snapped, and I realized that the series, EoE included, was brilliant and I needed to watch it all over again. Now it's one of my favorite TV shows of all time.

[]:             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Mon Jul 27, 2015 7:21 am IronEvangelion wrote: I know she has serious problems of her own now, but it's still no excuse for how she treats others.

[]:             Quote Post by Kamon-san » Mon Jul 27, 2015 8:18 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: After viewing The End of Evangelion for the first time, I hated the entire series from beginning to end. I hated Episode 1 all the way to both versions of Episode 26. Then after a couple of days either something clicked or I finally snapped, and I realized that the series, EoE included, was brilliant and I needed to watch it all over again. Now it's one of my favorite TV shows of all time.

[]:             Quote Post by Logoguy » Mon Jul 27, 2015 8:44 am NemZ wrote: My view of Asuka has changed from "dislike" to "dislike and pity". Does that count?

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Mon Jul 27, 2015 9:30 am I once liked the series and movie. But the more I learned about it the less I enjoyed it and eventually I ended up here.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Jul 27, 2015 9:56 am Logoguy wrote: I take it you're not a cat person?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Jul 27, 2015 11:16 am No house is complete without both.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Mon Jul 27, 2015 1:21 pm pwhodges wrote: No house is complete without both.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionGodMode » Mon Jul 27, 2015 1:30 pm I hated the whole 2nd half of end of evangelion, the shit microwaved my brain the first time. Also the movie as a whole seemed pointless with all the character dieing and Shinji ending the world and then starting over. But my 2nd rewatch i started to see Anno's abilities as a director to draw you into the world and sympthatize with the characters and understand their actions. Now its my favorite movie of all time and i love it.

[]:             Quote Post by Vegeta 20XX » Mon Jul 27, 2015 6:11 pm Shinji and Asuka (no, not what you're thinking, LAS) for their own reasons: *I hated Asuka with a passion - after all, it was blatantly obvious that Rei's everyday life was suffering while Asuka fixated on petty problems and social desirability. As time went on, I began to see Asuka's trademark "bitch" typecast as a symptom, not a cause; she suffers quite a bit too, just not in the same ways, or for the same reasons, but it all hurts nonetheless. *I always found some reason to fault Shinji for his shortcomings - from the "I'd do X better or differently" to "grow a spinesack". Still, I found that I kept coming back to the parallels between myself and the Third Child and eventually saw that only through the lens of adulthood can one see that Shinji handled everything he went through far better than any of us would have, and that most of us are here mostly because we are, in many respect, Shinji ourselves - not "we are like him" or "we have many similarities" - we ARE Shinji, minus the giant not-really-robots, thinly veiled Oedipus complexes, and planning, plotting jerkass dads, and I'm sure a few Shinji-identifiers have one or both of the last two.

[]:             Quote Post by LukeMM95 » Mon Jul 27, 2015 6:27 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: After viewing The End of Evnagelion for the first time, I hated the entire series from beginning to end. I hated Episode 1 all the way to both versions of Episode 26. Then after a couple of days either something clicked or I finally snapped, and I realized that the series, EoE included, was brilliant and I needed to watch it all over again. Now it's one of my favorite TV shows of all time.

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Mon Jul 27, 2015 7:05 pm I fucking hated the rebuild movies (1.11 and 2.22 specifically) on first watching, then for whatever reason a second watch and i loved them, 3.33 was something i loved from the get go.

[]:             Quote Post by IronEvangelion » Mon Jul 27, 2015 7:10 pm Arcadia's legacy wrote: Then, why is she my favorite character?

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Mon Jul 27, 2015 9:59 pm Rosenakahara wrote: I fucking hated the rebuild movies (1.11 and 2.22 specifically) on first watching, then for whatever reason a second watch and i loved them, 3.33 was something i loved from the get go.
